claimText: CAR-T therapy involves genetically modifying a patient's own T cells to add a Chimeric Antigen Receptor (CAR) enabling them to recognize and attack cancer cells.
evidenceExcerpt: Patient's own T cells are genetically modified with CAR to recognize and attack cancer cells.
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of CAR-T therapy.
distractorHooks: Using donor T cells instead of the patient's own T cells
Modifying B cells or stem cells instead of T cells
Using chemical drugs instead of living cells

claimText: Qartemi is a CAR T-cell therapy approved by the CDSCO specifically for treating blood cancer.
evidenceExcerpt: CDSCO approved Qartemi, a CAR T-cell therapy for treating blood cancer.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on recently approved biotechnology applications in India.
distractorHooks: Approved for treating solid tumors instead of blood cancer
Approved as a traditional chemotherapy drug
Approved by FDA instead of CDSCO

claimText: Cytokine Release Syndrome is a systemic inflammatory response that poses a significant challenge during CAR-T therapy.
evidenceExcerpt: Challenges include Cytokine Release Syndrome (a systemic inflammatory response)
examUse: Statement-based question on the side effects and challenges of advanced immunotherapies.
distractorHooks: Describing Cytokine Release Syndrome as a beneficial immune boost
Confusing it with an autoimmune disease caused by genetic mutation

claimText: Antigen Escape is a limitation of CAR-T therapy where tumors lose the targeted antigens, allowing them to evade the modified T cells.
evidenceExcerpt: Challenges include... Antigen Escape (tumors lose targeted antigens).
examUse: Statement-based question on the limitations of targeted cancer therapies.
distractorHooks: Defining antigen escape as the patient's body rejecting the infused T-cells
Stating that the modified T-cells lose their CAR receptors
